description: Figure 1 presents the integrated thermomechanical processing design and phase transformation validation for a high-Al (~5 wt.%) added low-density medium Mn steel (Fe-7.79Mn-4.88Al-0.84Si-0.2C). Panel (a) illustrates the schematic processing route after casting: homogenization at 1100°C for 2 hours, hot forging with 50% thickness reduction in the range of 1050-800°C followed by air cooling, hot rolling with soaking at 1100°C for 2 hours and two-pass 50% reduction (with 5-minute reheating between passes) followed by water quenching, and final intercritical annealing at 750°C for variable durations (5-180 minutes) with air cooling. Panel (b) displays the Thermo-Calc equilibrium phase fraction plot (TCFE9 database), revealing that the dual-phase region (BCC_A2 ferrite + FCC_A1 austenite) spans approximately 650°C to 1350°C, which provides the theoretical basis for selecting 750°C as the intercritical annealing temperature. Panel (c) shows dilatometry heating-cooling curves of hot-forged samples (5 and 15 minute holding times), exhibiting only a slight deviation around 600°C (marked by black ellipse) attributed to carbide dissolution, with no significant length contraction indicating minimal ferrite-to-austenite transformation due to the stability of delta ferrite. Collectively, these figures establish the computational and experimental foundation for optimizing the thermomechanical processing window to control austenite stability and phase transformations.
